reasoning: The series essentially flatlined starting around October 2025 (Oct 1: -242.9k, Jan 14: -243.7k), indicating the Fed's baseline operating net income has reached breakeven and the trend is now close to zero drift. The linear model predicted 0.29 based on the whole 6-month steep downward trend, but this is too pessimistic given the recent flatline. However, over the past three weeks the series dropped rapidly (from -242.1k to -243.7k), likely due to year-end accounting/accrual reversals generating short-term downward momentum. Therefore, the probability of an immediate recovery above -243.7k by the Jan 25 resolution date is lower than 50% (estimated at 0.38). By the Feb 17 resolution date, the random walk nature of the breakeven net income dominates, and positive net income may begin to accumulate, pulling the probability significantly closer to 0.5 as dictated by increasing uncertainty over the longer horizon.
